source_refs.quote: EXECUTION VERSION
Dated 24 December 2021 SENIOR FACILITIES AGREEMENT

deal_summary.purpose: Facility B refinances interim Facility B debt if drawn and otherwise finances/refinances acquisition consideration, Target shareholder payments, Existing Target Debt refinancing, Acquisition Costs, transaction-related payments, general corporate purposes and working capital. The Original Revolving Facility refinances any interim revolving facility if drawn and otherwise may be used for those purposes, general corporate/working capital, backstopping/cash cover for existing ancillary facilities, guarantees, letters of credit and other Target Group arrangements, and other transaction-related payments.
deal_summary.hierarchy_note: This is deal-level because Clause 2.1 makes available three facilities/tranches: Facility B (EUR), Facility B (USD) and the Original Revolving Facility, with further Additional Facility mechanics.

source_refs.title: The Facilities

source_refs.title: The Facilities
source_refs.quote: the Original Revolving Facility Lenders make available to the Original Revolving Facility Borrowers a multicurrency revolving credit facility

source_refs.title: Purpose

transaction_context.transaction_type: Acquisition financing for the acquisition of Target Shares by Bidco pursuant to a Scheme and/or Offer, with related refinancing of existing target debt and transaction costs.

acquisition.description: Acquisition means the acquisition of Target Shares by Bidco pursuant to a Scheme and/or Offer and, if applicable, a Squeeze-Out or other acquisition of Target Shares or related payments.

uses_of_proceeds: finance or refinance consideration for Target Shares
finance or refinance payments to shareholders of the Target
refinance or discharge Existing Target Debt
finance or refinance Acquisition Costs and related transaction amounts
general corporate purposes and working capital where proceeds are not otherwise applied

source_refs.quote: "Acquisition" means the acquisition of Target Shares by Bidco pursuant to a Scheme and/or Offer

Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation, London Branch

source_refs.quote: the aggregate outstanding principal amount of each Facility B (EUR) Loan borrowed by it in full on the Termination Date

instalments.date_description: Each Quarter Date commencing with the last day of the first full Financial Quarter ending after the utilisation date for that Facility B (USD) Loan
instalments.description: Quarterly amortisation equal to 0.25% of the aggregate outstanding principal amount of each Facility B (USD) Loan borrowed and not repaid or prepaid before the relevant Quarter Date.
Remaining aggregate outstanding principal amount due in full.

source_refs.title: Repayment of Facility B Loans
source_refs.quote: five (0.25) per cent. of the aggregate outstanding principal amount of each Facility B (USD) Loan borrowed by it

facility_interest_terms.reference_rate_name: IBOR / Compounded Reference Rate for applicable currency

interest_and_benchmark_terms.rfr_or_compounded_rate_mechanics: For Compounded Rate Currencies, the rate for each day during an Interest Period is the aggregate of Margin and the relevant Compounded Reference Rate for that day. Compounded Rate Terms are scheduled for Sterling and Swiss Francs.
interest_and_benchmark_terms.benchmark_replacement_mechanics: Clause 41.8 provides benchmark replacement mechanics; the Trigger Date with respect to USD is 30 June 2023, unless delayed publication cessation applies.

source_refs.title: Calculation of interest - Term Rate Loans
source_refs.quote: The rate of interest on each Term Rate Loan for an Interest Period is the percentage rate per annum which is the aggregate of the applicable: (a) Margin; and (b) IBOR.
in relation to any Facility B (EUR) Loan, 3.75 per cent. per annum

security_and_guarantees.guarantee_coverage: Each Guarantor irrevocably and unconditionally jointly and severally guarantees punctual performance by each Obligor, subject to Guarantee Limitations and Agreed Security Principles. The guarantee is continuing and extends to the ultimate balance of sums payable by any Obligor under the Finance Documents.

assets.description: Topco shares in the capital of the Company and Structural Intercompany Receivables
Company material bank accounts located in Luxembourg

Share pledges over Company shares in Holdco and Holdco shares in Bidco, bank account security for Holdco and Bidco material England bank accounts, security assignment over Structural Intercompany Receivables, and floating charges over Holdco and Bidco business and assets subject to exclusions
Company shares in the capital of US Holdco
Substantially all of US Holdco's assets, subject to customary exclusions and excluded asset language consistent with the Agreed Security Principles

security_documents: Luxembourg limited recourse security agreement by Topco
Luxembourg bank account pledge agreement by the Company
English law security agreement by Company / Holdco / Bidco
New York pledge agreement over Company shares in US Holdco
New York security agreement by US Holdco

intercreditor_and_subordination.ranking_summary: The Agreement is subject to and has the benefit of the Intercreditor Agreement, which prevails over the Agreement in case of inconsistency except for Clause 1.5. Additional Facility Lenders not already Lenders must accede to the Intercreditor Agreement in the relevant Senior Lender, Second Lien Lender, Topco Lender or post-Designation Date Super Senior Lender capacity.

source_refs.quote: This Agreement is subject to, and has the benefit of, the Intercreditor Agreement.

source_refs.title: Additional Facilities
source_refs.quote: the accession of each Additional Facility Lender to the Intercreditor Agreement in the capacity of a "Senior Lender", a "Second Lien Lender", a "Topco Lender" or (following the Designation Date) a "Super Senior Lender"

conditions_precedent.initial_conditions_precedent_ref: Schedule 2 Part I (Conditions Precedent to first Utilisation)

conditions.description: Constitutional documents, board approvals, shareholder resolutions where required, specimen signatures and formalities certificates for each Original Obligor and Topco.

Counterparts of Finance Documents signed by each Original Obligor and Topco where party: Intercreditor Agreement, Fee Letters and specified Transaction Security Documents subject to Agreed Security Principles.
Capacity and enforceability legal opinions from Luxembourg, New York and English counsel.
Reports including legal, financial, pensions, commercial, environmental, health and safety due diligence and tax structure memorandum, subject to no reliance as a condition precedent to funding.
Company certificate confirming the Scheme Effective Date or Offer Unconditional Date has occurred and minimum equity investment is not less than 42.5% of Total Transaction Uses.
Reasonable evidence of payment of all fees then due and payable to the Finance Parties for their own account under the Fee Letters, with invoice timing and offset qualifications.

source_refs.title: Conditions Precedent to first Utilisation
source_refs.quote: Conditions Precedent to first Utilisation

covenants.description: For the benefit of Lenders under each Financial Covenant Facility only, on any Test Date the Senior Secured Net Leverage Ratio as set out in the Compliance Certificate must not exceed 9.10:1, but only while any amount is outstanding under a Financial Covenant Facility and only if the Test Condition is met at 5.00 p.m. on the relevant Test Date.
covenants.threshold: Senior Secured Net Leverage Ratio not to exceed 9.10:1
covenants.test_frequency: Rolling basis for Testing Periods ending on relevant Test Dates and on delivery of Compliance Certificate, solely if Test Condition is met.

source_refs.title: Financial Condition
source_refs.quote: the Senior Secured Net Leverage Ratio as set out in the relevant Compliance Certificate shall not exceed 9.10:1

financial_covenants.testing_group: Group on a consolidated basis, as specified by the finance document calculation provisions.

source_refs.title: Financial Condition
source_refs.quote: the Financial Covenant shall not be required to be satisfied unless the Test Condition is met at 5.00 p.m. on any relevant Test Date

mandatory_prepayment_triggers.description: Change of Control provisions in Clause 12.1 apply as a mandatory prepayment/cancellation regime.

Excess Cash Flow prepayment percentage depends on the Senior Secured Net Leverage Ratio: greater than 4.45:1 equals 50%; equal to or less than 4.45:1 but greater than 4.20:1 equals 25%. Lower levels are described in Clause 12.2.

source_refs.quote: 11. Illegality, Voluntary Prepayment and Cancellation

source_refs.title: Excess Cash Flow
source_refs.quote: Greater than 4.45:1 Fifty (50) per cent.

additional_facility_and_accordion_mechan.mfn_margin_cap_description: Additional MFN Term Facility (EUR) and Additional MFN Term Facility (USD) margin caps equal 0.50% per annum plus the highest actual or potential Margin for the relevant Facility B tranche, subject to the definitions and Market Flex inclusion.
additional_facility_and_accordion_mechan.incremental_cap: Company may establish Additional Facilities from time to time if the applicable Clause 2.2 conditions are satisfied, including permitted indebtedness and specified maturity/amortisation conditions. Total Additional Facility Commitments are zero at the date of the Agreement.
additional_facility_and_accordion_mechan.mechanics_description: Company establishes an Additional Facility by delivering a duly completed Additional Facility Notice; no Finance Party consent is required other than from the Additional Facility Lenders making available the applicable facility, provided applicable conditions are met.

source_refs.title: Additional Facilities
source_refs.quote: the Company may, at any time and from time to time by delivering to the Agent a duly completed Additional Facility Notice

source_refs.reference: Additional MFN Term Facility (EUR) Margin Cap
source_refs.quote: zero point five (0.50) per cent. per annum; plus

source_refs.quote: an Ancillary Lender or Fronted Ancillary Lender and Fronting Ancillary Lender may make available an Ancillary Facility or a Fronted Ancillary Facility

amendments_and_waivers.general_consent_threshold: Majority Lenders and Company, subject to Clause 41 exceptions and the Intercreditor Agreement.

all_lender_matters.matter: Changes to definitions of Majority Lenders, Super Majority Lenders and Structural Adjustment
Changes to provisions expressly requiring all Lender consent
Specified Intercreditor Agreement waterfall priority changes adversely affecting Lenders, subject to carve-outs

amendments_and_waivers.technical_or_manifest_error_changes: Company and Agent or Security Agent may amend/waive without other Party consent to cure defects or omissions, resolve ambiguities or inconsistencies, reflect minor technical or administrative changes or manifest error, implement benefit-only or consequential changes subject to the clause limitations.
amendments_and_waivers.replacement_of_non_consenting_lenders: Clause 41.5 provides replacement mechanics for a Lender in connection with amendments/waivers and related actions.

source_refs.title: Required consents
source_refs.quote: be amended or waived only with the consent of the Majority Lenders and the Company

source_refs.title: Other exceptions
source_refs.quote: to cure defects or omissions; resolve ambiguities or inconsistencies

facility_agent.name: Credit Suisse AG, Cayman Islands Branch

payment_agent.name: Credit Suisse AG, Cayman Islands Branch

agency_payments_and_settlement.clawback_or_reversal_rights: If the Agent pays an amount to another Party and it proves the Agent had not actually received that amount, the recipient must on demand refund it with interest calculated to reflect the Agent's cost of funds.
agency_payments_and_settlement.payment_disruption_or_funding_indemnity: The Agreement includes Impaired Agent mechanics and Disruption Event mechanics for payment systems disruption, allowing direct or trust-account payments in certain cases and agreed operational changes.
agency_payments_and_settlement.set_off_terms: Finance Parties may set off matured obligations during a continuing Declared Default or, for Revolving Facility Lenders, a Declared RCF Default, subject to carve-outs for escrow, trust and tax accounts.
agency_payments_and_settlement.currency_of_account_rules: Base Currency is currency of account and payment except repayments and interest are in the currency of the relevant Utilisation or Unpaid Sum, and costs/taxes in the currency incurred unless otherwise agreed.

source_refs.title: Payments to the Agent
source_refs.quote: that Obligor or Lender shall make the same available to the Agent

source_refs.title: Partial payments
source_refs.quote: first, in or towards payment pro rata of any unpaid fees, costs and expenses of the Agent

normal_course_payment_waterfall.recipient_class: Agent, Issuing Banks and Security Agent

normal_course_payment_waterfall.description: Accrued interest, fee or commission due but unpaid
Principal due but unpaid and unpaid Letter of Credit claim/indemnity amounts

normal_course_payment_waterfall.description: Any other sum due but unpaid under the Finance Documents

cash_waterfalls.sharing_provisions: Clause 34 provides sharing among Finance Parties, with carve-outs including Ancillary Lender receipts before acceleration.

source_refs.title: Partial payments
source_refs.quote: the Agent shall apply that payment towards the obligations of that Obligor under those Finance Documents in the following order

deemed_delivery_rules: Email effective when received in legible form.
Letter effective when left at the relevant address or five Business Days after being deposited in the post postage prepaid in an envelope addressed to that address.
Communications to Agent or Security Agent are effective only when actually received and expressly marked for required attention.
notice_mechanics.change_of_details_mechanics: Substitute address, email address, department or officer may be notified by not less than five Business Days' notice; Agent must promptly notify other Parties of details changes.

source_refs.title: Communications in writing
source_refs.quote: may be made by electronic mail or letter

source_refs.title: Delivery
source_refs.quote: if by way of electronic mail, when received in legible form

extraction_notes: Full PDF text extracted to data/07_deal_ultra_group_senior_facilities.json.source.txt before JSON extraction.
Facility amounts use facility currencies and original lender commitments reconcile to Schedule 1 Part II totals.
Termination dates depend on the Initial Utilisation Date, which is not stated as an absolute date in the extracted agreement; therefore absolute termination_date fields were not populated.
Source defines Base Currency per facility (EUR for Facility B (EUR), USD for Facility B (USD), Sterling for the Original Revolving Facility); no single deal-level base_currency is populated.
known_omissions: Full text of Schedule 15 undertakings, Schedule 16 Events of Default and Schedule 17 New York law defined terms was not exhaustively captured because the deal schema supports summaries and source references for those blocks.
Optional currency list for the revolving facility is based on benchmark/currency definitions and may not be exhaustive of all optional currency approvals or mechanics.
